зҺ°еңЁиҝҷдёӘйЎ№зӣ®е·Із»Ҹд»ҺвҖңжҠҠ `Memory/` ж”ҫеңЁејҖеҸ‘зӣ®еҪ•йҮҢйЎәжүӢеҗҢжӯҘвҖқзҡ„е·Ҙе…·пјҢж”¶еҸЈжҲҗдәҶдёҖдёӘд»Ҙ `memorytree` еҲҶж”ҜдёәеҚ•дёҖзңҹжәҗзҡ„йЎ№зӣ®и®°еҝҶзі»з»ҹгҖӮ

**йЎ№зӣ®зҺ°еңЁзҡ„е®ҡдҪҚ**
жҢүйӮЈ 10 йЎ№дҝ®еӨҚеҗҺзҡ„з»“жһңзңӢпјҢMemoryTree Workflow зҺ°еңЁдё»иҰҒеҒҡ 4 д»¶дәӢпјҡ

1. з»ҷд»“еә“е»әз«ӢеҸҜжҢҒз»ӯзҡ„йЎ№зӣ®и®°еҝҶеұӮ
2. жҠҠ AI дјҡиҜқ transcript еҸҳжҲҗеҸҜжЈҖзҙўгҖҒеҸҜеӣһеҝҶгҖҒеҸҜеҸ‘еёғзҡ„зҹҘиҜҶиө„дә§
3. з”ЁзӢ¬з«Ӣ worktree + зӢ¬з«ӢеҲҶж”Ҝе®үе…Ёең°и·‘иҮӘеҠЁеҢ– heartbeat
4. и®©ејҖеҸ‘зӣ®еҪ•еҸӘжүҝжӢ…вҖңжң¬ең°зј“еӯҳй•ңеғҸвҖқзҡ„иҒҢиҙЈпјҢдёҚеҶҚжүҝжӢ…е…ұдә«и®°еҝҶзңҹжәҗ

еҜ№еә”иҜҙжҳҺе·Із»Ҹз»ҹдёҖеңЁ [README.md](/d:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/README.md#L126) е’Ң [worktree-heartbeat-upgrade-plan.md](/d:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/docs/worktree-heartbeat-upgrade-plan.md#L184)гҖӮ

**зҺ°еңЁзҡ„ж ёеҝғеҠҹиғҪ**
1. д»“еә“жҺҘе…ҘдёҺеҲқе§ӢеҢ–
- `memorytree init` / `memorytree upgrade` иҙҹиҙЈеҲӣе»әжҲ–иЎҘйҪҗ `AGENTS.md` е’Ң `Memory/` з»“жһ„
- е®ғд»¬дёҚеҶҚй»ҳи®ӨжӣҝдҪ жіЁеҶҢ heartbeatпјҢжҺҘе…ҘеҗҺеҸ°д»»еҠЎиҰҒиө° `daemon quick-start` жҲ– `daemon register`
- иҝҷйғЁеҲҶе…ҘеҸЈеңЁ [main.ts](/d:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/src/cli/main.ts:421) е’Ң [README.md](/d:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/README.md#L51)

2. Transcript еҜје…ҘдёҺеҪ’жЎЈ
- ж”ҜжҢҒ `discover`гҖҒ`import`гҖҒ`recall`
- transcript дјҡжӢҶжҲҗ rawгҖҒcleanгҖҒmanifestпјҢе№¶иҝӣе…Ҙе…ЁеұҖеҪ’жЎЈ `~/.memorytree/`
- еҪ“еүҚд»“еә“еҸӘй•ңеғҸвҖңеұһдәҺжң¬йЎ№зӣ®вҖқзҡ„ transcriptпјҢи·ЁйЎ№зӣ®еҶ…е®№з•ҷеңЁе…ЁеұҖжЎЈжЎҲ
- иҝҷдҝқиҜҒ recall е’Ң report йғҪжңүзЁіе®ҡж•°жҚ®жәҗ

3. Report з”ҹжҲҗдёҺжҹҘзңӢ
- иғҪжҠҠ Memory еҶ…е®№з”ҹжҲҗйқҷжҖҒ HTML з«ҷзӮ№еҲ° `Memory/07_reports`
- еҸҜд»Ҙдёҙж—¶ `report serve`
- д№ҹеҸҜд»Ҙиө° Caddy еҒҡй•ҝжңҹжң¬ең°жүҳз®ЎпјҢиҝҳж”ҜжҢҒ GitHub Pages / webhook
- зӣёе…іе…ҘеҸЈеңЁ [README.md](/d:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/README.md#L326) е’Ң [main.ts](/d:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/src/cli/main.ts:178)

4. Heartbeat иҮӘеҠЁеҢ–
- зҺ°еңЁ heartbeat зҡ„е”ҜдёҖиҠӮеҘҸе°ұжҳҜ `heartbeat_interval`
- `refresh_interval` е·Із»ҸйҖҖеҮә runtime / CLI / config иЎЁйқў
- жҜҸж¬Ў heartbeat зҡ„зЁіе®ҡйЎәеәҸжҳҜпјҡ
  `sync context -> import -> build report -> commit/push -> sync outputs back`
- ж ёеҝғйҖ»иҫ‘еңЁ [heartbeat.ts](/d:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/src/heartbeat/heartbeat.ts:84)

5. Worktree еҚ•дёҖзңҹжәҗ
- ејҖеҸ‘зӣ®еҪ•йҮҢзҡ„ `AGENTS.md` е’Ң `Memory/01-05` жҳҜвҖңжҙ»еҠЁдёҠдёӢж–ҮвҖқ
- heartbeat иҝҗиЎҢеүҚе…ҲжҠҠиҝҷдәӣеҗҢжӯҘеҲ° memory worktree
- `Memory/06-07` еңЁ worktree дёӯз”ҹжҲҗеҗҺпјҢеҶҚеҗҢжӯҘеӣһејҖеҸ‘зӣ®еҪ•
- еҗҢжӯҘеҮҪж•°еңЁ [sync.ts](/d:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/src/heartbeat/sync.ts:35) е’Ң [sync.ts](/d:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/src/heartbeat/sync.ts:43)

6. Git е®үе…ЁдёҺиҮӘеҠЁжҸҗдәӨ
- иҮӘеҠЁ repo commit еҸӘеҸ‘з”ҹеңЁ dedicated memory branch
- йқһ memory еҲҶж”ҜдҝқжҢҒд»“еә“е№ІеҮҖпјҢдёҚеҒҡ repo-local иҮӘеҠЁжҸҗдәӨ
- еҚідҪҝејҖеҸ‘зӣ®еҪ•жҠҠ `AGENTS.md` / `Memory/**` йҖҖи·ҹиёӘжҲ– ignoreпјҢmemory еҲҶж”Ҝд»ҚиғҪжҸҗдәӨжүҳз®ЎеҶ…е®№
- raw transcript жҳҜеҗҰе…Ғи®ёжҸҗдәӨпјҢз”ұ `raw_upload_permission` жҺ§еҲ¶
- иҝҷйғЁеҲҶи§„еҲҷеңЁ [README.md](/d:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/README.md#L403)

**10 йЎ№дҝ®еӨҚеёҰжқҘзҡ„е®һиҙЁеҸҳеҢ–**
з»“еҗҲдҪ йӮЈд»Ҫ [и®°еҝҶеҲҶж”ҜеҚ•дёҖзңҹжәҗдёӨйҳ¶ж®өж”№йҖ ж–№жЎҲ.md](/d:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/docs/и®°еҝҶеҲҶж”ҜеҚ•дёҖзңҹжәҗдёӨйҳ¶ж®өж”№йҖ ж–№жЎҲ.md)пјҢеҸҜд»Ҙжө“зј©жҲҗиҝҷеҮ жқЎпјҡ

1. дә§е“ҒиҜӯд№үеҶ»з»“дәҶ
- дёҚеҶҚжҠҠејҖеҸ‘зӣ®еҪ•е’Ң memory еҲҶж”ҜйғҪиҜҙжҲҗвҖңе…ұдә«и®°еҝҶеұӮвҖқ
- дёҚеҶҚжҠҠ `30m refresh` еҪ“ж ёеҝғиғҪеҠӣ

2. зңҹжәҗй—ӯзҺҜиЎҘйҪҗдәҶ
- `memorytree` еҲҶж”ҜзҺ°еңЁжүҝжҺҘе®Ңж•ҙеҝ«з…§пјҢдёҚеҶҚеҸӘжҸҗдәӨдёҖйғЁеҲҶ

3. heartbeat йЎәеәҸзЁіе®ҡдәҶ
- е…ҲеҗҢжӯҘдёҠдёӢж–ҮпјҢеҶҚеҜје…ҘпјҢеҶҚжһ„е»әжҠҘе‘ҠпјҢеҶҚжҸҗдәӨпјҢеҶҚеӣһеҶҷзј“еӯҳ

4. ејҖеҸ‘зӣ®еҪ•и§’иүІеҸҳдәҶ
- е®ғжҳҜжң¬ең°зј“еӯҳй•ңеғҸпјҢдёҚжҳҜе…ұдә«зңҹжәҗ
- иҝҷи®©дё»ејҖеҸ‘еҲҶж”Ҝжӣҙе№ІеҮҖпјҢиҜҜжҸҗдәӨжөҒзЁӢйЈҺйҷ©жӣҙдҪҺ

5. й…ҚзҪ®жЁЎеһӢз®ҖеҢ–дәҶ
- з”ЁжҲ·зҺ°еңЁеҸӘйңҖиҰҒзҗҶи§Ј heartbeat cadence
- дёҚйңҖиҰҒеҶҚйўқеӨ–зҗҶи§Ј `refresh_interval`

assistant #857 final_answer 08:27:38

жҲ‘з”Ё memorytree-workflow жҹҘиҝҮдәҶпјҢз»“и®әжҳҜиҝҷж®ө openfons иҫ“еҮәдёҚжҳҜеҪ“еүҚиҝҷдёӘд»“еә“ memorytree-workflow зҡ„е®һж—¶зҠ¶жҖҒпјҢиҖҢжҳҜеҸҰдёҖдёӘйЎ№зӣ®/ж—§дјҡиҜқзҡ„еӣһж”ҫпјҢжүҖд»ҘйҮҢйқўзҡ„ Refresh interval: 30m дёҚиғҪзӣҙжҺҘжӢҝжқҘеҪ“зҺ°еңЁиҝҷд»Ҫд»“еә“зҡ„зҠ¶жҖҒзңӢгҖӮ

дҪ е…іеҝғзҡ„дёӨзӮ№пјҢеҪ“еүҚд»“еә“йҮҢе…¶е®һе·Із»ҸеҜ№йҪҗдәҶпјҡ
- Memory/ е·Із»ҸеңЁ [.gitignore](/D:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/.gitignore:8) йҮҢиў«еҝҪз•ҘдәҶпјҢеҪ“еүҚи§„еҲҷе°ұжҳҜ AGENTS.md + Memory/**пјҢжүҖд»Ҙдё»зӣ®еҪ•зҡ„ Memory дҪңдёәзј“еӯҳй•ңеғҸдёҚиҜҘеҶҚиҝӣжҷ®йҖҡжҸҗдәӨжөҒгҖӮ
- refresh_interval зҺ°еңЁе·Із»ҸйҖҖеҮәиҝҗиЎҢж—¶иҜӯд№үдәҶпјҢREADME йҮҢжҳҺзЎ®еҶҷдәҶе®ғвҖңдёҚеҶҚжҳҜ runtime/config зҡ„дёҖйғЁеҲҶвҖқ [README.md](/D:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/README.md:304)пјҢCLI еҪ“еүҚд№ҹеҸӘиҫ“еҮә Heartbeat interval е’Ң Auto-pushпјҢдёҚеҶҚжҡҙйңІ refresh interval [cmd-daemon.ts](/D:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/src/cli/cmd-daemon.ts:226)гҖӮ

жҲ‘иҝҳд»ҺжңҖиҝ‘зҡ„дјҡиҜқйҮҢе®ҡдҪҚеҲ°дәҶдҪ иҜҙзҡ„йӮЈжқЎвҖң10 йЎ№дҝ®еӨҚдё»зәҝвҖқпјҢе®ғеңЁ 03-24 зҡ„жҖ»з»“йҮҢе·Із»Ҹж”¶еҸЈдәҶпјҢйЎ№зӣ®зҺ°еңЁиў«еҪ’зәіжҲҗ 4 д»¶дәӢпјҡ[transcript](/C:/Users/ai/.memorytree/transcripts/clean/codex/memorytree-workflow/2026/03/019d1919-a676-7753-a820-7778a5697bb4__956b15f9.md:2599) е’Ң [transcript](/C:/Users/ai/.memorytree/transcripts/clean/codex/memorytree-workflow/2026/03/019d1919-a676-7753-a820-7778a5697bb4__956b15f9.md:2637)гҖӮ

еҰӮжһңдҪ ж„ҝж„ҸпјҢжҲ‘дёӢдёҖжӯҘеҸҜд»ҘзӣҙжҺҘжҠҠйӮЈ 10 йЎ№жҢүвҖңй—®йўҳ - и§ЈеҶіж–№жЎҲ - еҪ“еүҚжҳҜеҗҰе·Іж”¶еҸЈвҖқж•ҙзҗҶжҲҗдёҖеј иЎЁз»ҷдҪ гҖӮ

assistant #870 final_answer 08:36:02

жҳҺзҷҪпјҢдҪ иҰҒзңӢзҡ„жҳҜиҝҷдёӘ skill жң¬иә«зҡ„иғҪеҠӣпјҢдёҚжҳҜеҪ“еүҚд»“еә“зҡ„зҠ¶жҖҒгҖӮ

иҝҷдёӘ memorytree-workflow skill зҡ„вҖңеҝ«йҖҹй…ҚзҪ®вҖқиғҪеҠӣпјҢж ёеҝғе°ұжҳҜз»ҷдёҖдёӘж–°йЎ№зӣ®дёҖй”®жҺҘе…Ҙ MemoryTreeпјҡ
- е…ҲжҠҠиҝҷеҸ°жңәеҷЁдёҠзҡ„еҝғи·іи°ғеәҰеҷЁеҮҶеӨҮеҘҪ
- еҶҚжҠҠеҪ“еүҚд»“еә“жіЁеҶҢиҝӣеҺ»
- з„¶еҗҺз«ӢеҚіи·‘дёҖж¬ЎеҗҢжӯҘпјҢеҒҡ transcript еҜје…ҘгҖҒжҠҘе‘Ҡз”ҹжҲҗгҖҒеҝ…иҰҒж—¶зҡ„жҸҗдәӨ/жҺЁйҖҒ

жүҖд»ҘдҪ еҲҡжүҚзңӢеҲ°зҡ„йӮЈз§Қз»“жһңпјҢеә”иҜҘзҗҶи§ЈжҲҗвҖңж–°йЎ№зӣ® quick-start еҗҺзҡ„ж ҮеҮҶиҗҪең°еҪўжҖҒвҖқпјҢиҖҢдёҚжҳҜвҖңеҸӘжҳҜиЈ…дәҶдёҖдёӘе®ҡж—¶еҷЁвҖқгҖӮ

е®ғзҡ„иғҪеҠӣиҫ№з•Ңд№ҹеҫҲжҳҺзЎ®пјҡ
- daemon install еҸӘз®ЎжңәеҷЁзә§и°ғеәҰеҷЁ
- daemon quick-start жүҚжҳҜвҖңж–°йЎ№зӣ®еҝ«йҖҹжҺҘе…ҘвҖқзҡ„дёҖй”®и·Ҝеҫ„
- daemon register жҳҜжӣҙз»Ҷзҡ„иҮӘе®ҡд№үжҺҘе…Ҙ
- init / upgrade иҙҹиҙЈд»“еә“йҮҢзҡ„ MemoryTree з»“жһ„иЎҘйҪҗ

иҝҷдёӘ memorytree-workflow skill зҡ„вҖңеҝ«йҖҹй…ҚзҪ®вҖқиғҪеҠӣпјҢж ёеҝғе°ұжҳҜз»ҷдёҖдёӘж–°йЎ№зӣ®дёҖй”®жҺҘе…Ҙ MemoryTreeпјҡ
- е…ҲжҠҠиҝҷеҸ°жңәеҷЁдёҠзҡ„еҝғи·іи°ғеәҰеҷЁеҮҶеӨҮеҘҪ
- еҶҚжҠҠеҪ“еүҚд»“еә“жіЁеҶҢиҝӣеҺ»
- з„¶еҗҺз«ӢеҚіи·‘дёҖж¬ЎеҗҢжӯҘпјҢеҒҡ transcript еҜје…ҘгҖҒжҠҘе‘Ҡз”ҹжҲҗгҖҒеҝ…иҰҒж—¶зҡ„жҸҗдәӨ/жҺЁйҖҒ

жүҖд»ҘдҪ еҲҡжүҚзңӢеҲ°зҡ„йӮЈз§Қз»“жһңпјҢеә”иҜҘзҗҶи§ЈжҲҗвҖңж–°йЎ№зӣ® quick-start еҗҺзҡ„ж ҮеҮҶиҗҪең°еҪўжҖҒвҖқпјҢиҖҢдёҚжҳҜвҖңеҸӘжҳҜиЈ…дәҶдёҖдёӘе®ҡж—¶еҷЁвҖқгҖӮ

е®ғзҡ„иғҪеҠӣиҫ№з•Ңд№ҹеҫҲжҳҺзЎ®пјҡ
- daemon install еҸӘз®ЎжңәеҷЁзә§и°ғеәҰеҷЁ
- daemon quick-start жүҚжҳҜвҖңж–°йЎ№зӣ®еҝ«йҖҹжҺҘе…ҘвҖқзҡ„дёҖй”®и·Ҝеҫ„
- daemon register жҳҜжӣҙз»Ҷзҡ„иҮӘе®ҡд№үжҺҘе…Ҙ
- init / upgrade иҙҹиҙЈд»“еә“йҮҢзҡ„ MemoryTree з»“жһ„иЎҘйҪҗ

Using Node v20.20.1
Using Node v20.20.1

### Use the skill

In Claude Code, run:

```text
/memorytree-workflow
```

The skill detects whether the current repository is `not-installed`, `partial`, or `installed`, then takes the next appropriate step immediately.

### Use the CLI directly

`memorytree init` and `memorytree upgrade` only create or update repository files. They do not register the repository with heartbeat or modify `~/.memorytree/config.toml` by themselves.

Recommended first-time setup for a repository that should use heartbeat:

```bash
memorytree upgrade --root . --format json
memorytree daemon quick-start --root .
memorytree caddy enable --root .
```

Quick Start keeps the dedicated `memorytree` branch as the shared source of truth and refreshes `AGENTS.md` plus `Memory/01_goals` through `Memory/05_archive` into your development directory as local cache mirrors, while `Memory/06_transcripts` and `Memory/07_reports` flow back from the memory worktree.
Its recommended defaults are `memorytree`, `heartbeat_interval = "5m"`, `auto_push = true`, `generate_report = true`, and `raw_upload_permission = "not-set"`, so raw transcript mirror commits stay disabled until you explicitly approve them.

Useful day-to-day commands:

```bash
memorytree recall --root .
memorytree discover --root . --client all --scope current-project --format json
memorytree report build --root . --no-ai --locale en
memorytree caddy status --root .
memorytree daemon run-once --root . --force
```

## Architecture

MemoryTree now runs as a worktree-aware single-source system, not just a single-folder transcript importer:

```text
Development repository (local cache mirror)
  AGENTS.md
  Memory/01_goals ... Memory/05_archive
        |
        | sync active context into
        v
Dedicated MemoryTree worktree (shared source of truth)
  isolated memorytree branch
  Memory/06_transcripts
  Memory/07_reports

Exit code: 0
Wall time: 0.4 seconds
Output:
Using Node v20.20.1
Using Node v20.20.1
## Heartbeat

The heartbeat is the background automation layer. It is now project-aware and worktree-aware.

Recommended setup:

```bash
memorytree daemon quick-start --root .
```

Use this as the quick-install path when you want the current repository to join heartbeat with the recommended defaults.

Quick Start keeps the dedicated `memorytree` branch as the shared source of truth and treats your development directory as a local cache mirror.
Its recommended defaults are `memorytree`, `heartbeat_interval = "5m"`, `auto_push = true`, `generate_report = true`, and `raw_upload_permission = "not-set"`.

If you want more control, install the machine scheduler once and then register each repository with your own branch, heartbeat cadence, and report settings:

```bash
memorytree daemon install --interval 5m --auto-push true
memorytree daemon register --root . --branch memorytree-docs --heartbeat-interval 10m --auto-push false --generate-report true --report-port 10010
memorytree daemon run-once --root . --force
```

The heartbeat now drives both transcript import and cache-mirror refresh timing. `refresh_interval` is no longer part of the runtime or config surface.

Current execution flow:

```text
Acquire lock
  -> load config
  -> select due projects
  -> ensure dedicated MemoryTree worktree
  -> sync AGENTS.md + Memory/01_goals through Memory/05_archive into the worktree
  -> discover and import matching transcripts
  -> build/update Memory/07_reports when report generation is enabled
  -> commit/push Memory/01-05 changes on the MemoryTree branch when allowed
  -> sync Memory/06_transcripts and Memory/07_reports back to the development directory
  -> release lock
```

Important branch-safety rules:

*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: D:\demo1\memorytree-workflow\README.md
@@
 ## Quick Start
 
-### Install the skill
-
-1. Install Node.js 20 or newer.
-2. Clone this repository into your Codex skills directory.
-3. Run `npm install`, `npm run build`, and `npm link` in the skill repository.
-4. Open any target repository and run `memorytree daemon quick-start --root .`.
+### Install the skill
+
+`memorytree-workflow` is a Codex skill. Install it once on your machine, then use it in any target repository.
+
+1. Install Node.js 20 or newer.
+2. Clone this repository into your Codex skills directory.
+3. Build and link the skill locally.
+
+Windows PowerShell:
+
+```powershell
+cd C:\Users\ai\.codex\skills\memorytree-workflow
+npm install
+npm run build
+npm link
+```
+
+macOS:
+
+```bash
+cd ~/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow
+npm install
+npm run build
+npm link
+```
+
+Ubuntu:
+
+```bash
+cd ~/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow
+npm install
+npm run build
+npm link
+```
+
+4. Open any target repository and run:
+
+```bash
+memorytree daemon quick-start --root .
+```
+
+That command is the same on Windows, macOS, and Ubuntu.
 
 ### Use the skill
 
 In Claude Code, run:
@@
 ## Heartbeat
 
 The heartbeat is the background automation layer. It is now project-aware and worktree-aware.
 
 Recommended setup:
 
 ```bash
 memorytree daemon quick-start --root .
 ```
 
-Use this as the quick-install path when you want the current repository to join heartbeat with the recommended defaults.
+Use this as the quick-install path when you want the current repository to join heartbeat with the recommended defaults. The command itself is cross-platform; the only OS-specific part is the one-time skill installation above.
*** End Patch

Output:
Using Node v20.20.1
Using Node v20.20.1
- A dedicated MemoryTree worktree that the heartbeat can update safely
- A global archive under `~/.memorytree/` for cross-project transcript storage and indexing

That model lets MemoryTree keep your main development flow clean while still importing transcripts, generating reports, and optionally publishing Memory-only updates from an isolated branch.

## Quick Start

### Install as a skill

Claude Code:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow ~/.claude/skills/memorytree-workflow
cd ~/.claude/skills/memorytree-workflow
npm install
npm run build
```

Codex:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow ~/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow
cd ~/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow
npm install
npm run build
```

Windows PowerShell (Codex):

```powershell
git clone https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow.git `
  $env:USERPROFILE\.codex\skills\memorytree-workflow

cd $env:USERPROFILE\.codex\skills\memorytree-workflow
npm install
npm run build
```

If your installer expects a skill path, use the repository root. `SKILL.md` lives at the top level.

Optional: expose the CLI as `memorytree` in your shell:

```bash
npm link
```
Tool Result #899 shell_command call_SCs2m8nPjKpCPxRjRGM8HY6L exit 0 08:41:52
shell_command output=Exit code: 0 Wall time: 0.4 seconds Output: Using Node v20.20.1 Using Node v20.20.1 ## Heartbeat The heartbeat is the background automation layer. It is now project-aware and wo...
Show output payload 
Exit code: 0
Wall time: 0.4 seconds
Output:
Using Node v20.20.1
Using Node v20.20.1
## Heartbeat

The heartbeat is the background automation layer. It is now project-aware and worktree-aware.

Recommended setup:

```bash
memorytree daemon quick-start --root .
```

Use this as the quick-install path when you want the current repository to join heartbeat with the recommended defaults.

Quick Start keeps the dedicated `memorytree` branch as the shared source of truth and treats your development directory as a local cache mirror.
Its recommended defaults are `memorytree`, `heartbeat_interval = "5m"`, `auto_push = true`, `generate_report = true`, and `raw_upload_permission = "not-set"`.

If you want more control, install the machine scheduler once and then register each repository with your own branch, heartbeat cadence, and report settings:

```bash
memorytree daemon install --interval 5m --auto-push true
memorytree daemon register --root . --branch memorytree-docs --heartbeat-interval 10m --auto-push false --generate-report true --report-port 10010
memorytree daemon run-once --root . --force
```

The heartbeat now drives both transcript import and cache-mirror refresh timing. `refresh_interval` is no longer part of the runtime or config surface.

Current execution flow:

```text
Acquire lock
  -> load config
  -> select due projects

Using Node v20.20.1
Using Node v20.20.1
## Quick Start

### Install as a skill

Install the skill once on your machine, then use it from any repository.

You need Node.js 20 or newer.

Windows PowerShell (Codex):

```powershell
git clone https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow "$env:USERPROFILE\.codex\skills\memorytree-workflow"
cd "$env:USERPROFILE\.codex\skills\memorytree-workflow"
npm install
npm run build
npm link
```

macOS (Claude Code or Codex):

```bash
git clone https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow ~/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow
cd ~/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow
npm install
npm run build
npm link
```

Ubuntu (Claude Code or Codex):

```bash
git clone https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow ~/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow
cd ~/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow
npm install
npm run build
npm link
```

If your skill installer expects a local path instead of a git URL, point it at the repository root. `SKILL.md` lives at the top level.

After the skill is linked, open any target repository and run:

```bash
memorytree daemon quick-start --root .
```

That `quick-start` command is the same on Windows, macOS, and Ubuntu.

### Use the skill

In Claude Code, run:

```text
/memorytree-workflow
```

Using Node v20.20.1

## Heartbeat

The heartbeat is the background automation layer. It is now project-aware and worktree-aware.

Recommended setup:

```bash
memorytree daemon quick-start --root .
```

Use this as the quick-install path when you want the current repository to join heartbeat with the recommended defaults.

Quick Start keeps the dedicated `memorytree` branch as the shared source of truth and treats your development directory as a local cache mirror.
Its recommended defaults are `memorytree`, `heartbeat_interval = "5m"`, `auto_push = true`, `generate_report = true`, and `raw_upload_permission = "not-set"`.

If you want more control, install the machine scheduler once and then register each repository with your own branch, heartbeat cadence, and report settings:

```bash
memorytree daemon install --interval 5m --auto-push true
memorytree daemon register --root . --branch memorytree-docs --heartbeat-interval 10m --auto-push false --generate-report true --report-port 10010
memorytree daemon run-once --root . --force
```

`memorytree daemon install` is the machine-level step, while `memorytree daemon quick-start --root .` is the one-command repository setup. Both are cross-platform; only the initial skill installation differs by OS.

The heartbeat now drives both transcript import and cache-mirror refresh timing. `refresh_interval` is no longer part of the runtime or config surface.

-git clone https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow.git `
-  $env:USERPROFILE\.codex\skills\memorytree-workflow
-
-cd $env:USERPROFILE\.codex\skills\memorytree-workflow
+```bash
+git clone https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow ~/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow
+cd ~/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow
 npm install
 npm run build
+npm link
 ```
 
-If your installer expects a skill path, use the repository root. `SKILL.md` lives at the top level.
+If you use Claude Code, replace `.codex` with `.claude` in the path above. If your skill installer expects a local path instead of a git URL, point it at the repository root. `SKILL.md` lives at the top level.
 
-Optional: expose the CLI as `memorytree` in your shell:
+After the skill is linked, open any target repository and run:
 
 ```bash
-npm link
+memorytree daemon quick-start --root .
 ```
 
+That `quick-start` command is the same on Windows, macOS, and Ubuntu.
+
 ### Use the skill
 
 In Claude Code, run:
@@ -301,6 +308,8 @@ memorytree daemon register --root . --branch memorytree-docs --heartbeat-interva
 memorytree daemon run-once --root . --force
 ```
 
+`memorytree daemon install` is the machine-level step, while `memorytree daemon quick-start --root .` is the one-command repository setup. Both are cross-platform; only the initial skill installation differs by OS.
+
 The heartbeat now drives both transcript import and cache-mirror refresh timing. `refresh_interval` is no longer part of the runtime or config surface.

 ### Install as a skill
 
-Claude Code:
+Install the skill once on your machine, then use it from any repository.
 
-```bash
-git clone https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow ~/.claude/skills/memorytree-workflow
-cd ~/.claude/skills/memorytree-workflow
+You need Node.js 20 or newer.
+
+Windows PowerShell:
+
+```powershell
+git clone https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow "$env:USERPROFILE\.codex\skills\memorytree-workflow"
+cd "$env:USERPROFILE\.codex\skills\memorytree-workflow"
 npm install
 npm run build
+npm link
 ```
 
-Codex:
+macOS:
 
 ```bash
 git clone https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow ~/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow
 cd ~/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow
 npm install
 npm run build
+npm link
 ```
 
-Windows PowerShell (Codex):
+Ubuntu:
 
-```powershell
-git clone https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow.git `
-  $env:USERPROFILE\.codex\skills\memorytree-workflow
-
-cd $env:USERPROFILE\.codex\skills\memorytree-workflow

+git clone https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow ~/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow
+cd ~/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow

 npm run build
+npm link
 ```
 
-If your installer expects a skill path, use the repository root. `SKILL.md` lives at the top level.
+If you use Claude Code, replace `.codex` with `.claude` in the path above. If your skill installer expects a local path instead of a git URL, point it at the repository root. `SKILL.md` lives at the top level.
 
-Optional: expose the CLI as `memorytree` in your shell:
+After the skill is linked, open any target repository and run:
 
 ```bash
-npm link
+memorytree daemon quick-start --root .
 ```
 
+That `quick-start` command is the same on Windows, macOS, and Ubuntu.
+
 ### Use the skill
 
 In Claude Code, run:
@@ -301,6 +308,8 @@ memorytree daemon register --root . --branch memorytree-docs --heartbeat-interva
 memorytree daemon run-once --root . --force
 ```
 
+`memorytree daemon install` is the machine-level step, while `memorytree daemon quick-start --root .` is the one-command repository setup. Both are cross-platform; only the initial skill installation differs by OS.
+
 The heartbeat now drives both transcript import and cache-mirror refresh timing. `refresh_interval` is no longer part of the runtime or config surface.

Tool Result #998 shell_command call_sHipXYuQXHowQrY2lFFqo5ut exit 0 08:51:13

Using Node v20.20.1
Using Node v20.20.1
### Install as a skill

Install the skill once on your machine, then use it from any repository.

You need Node.js 20 or newer.

Windows PowerShell:

```powershell
git clone https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow "$env:USERPROFILE\.codex\skills\memorytree-workflow"
cd "$env:USERPROFILE\.codex\skills\memorytree-workflow"
npm install
npm run build
npm link
```

macOS:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow ~/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow
cd ~/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow
npm install
npm run build
npm link
```

Ubuntu:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow ~/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow
cd ~/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow
npm install
npm run build
npm link
```

If you use Claude Code, replace `.codex` with `.claude` in the path above. If your skill installer expects a local path instead of a git URL, point it at the repository root. `SKILL.md` lives at the top level.

After the skill is linked, open any target repository and run:

```bash
memorytree daemon quick-start --root .
```
Tool Result #999 shell_command call_mF4bjGBChphFhRDRET8rFP2A exit 0 08:51:13
shell_command output=Exit code: 0 Wall time: 0.5 seconds Output: Using Node v20.20.1 Using Node v20.20.1 ## Heartbeat The heartbeat is the background automation layer. It is now project-aware and wo...
Show output payload 
Exit code: 0
Wall time: 0.5 seconds
Output:
Using Node v20.20.1
Using Node v20.20.1

## Heartbeat

The heartbeat is the background automation layer. It is now project-aware and worktree-aware.

Recommended setup:

```bash
memorytree daemon quick-start --root .
```

Use this as the quick-install path when you want the current repository to join heartbeat with the recommended defaults.

Quick Start keeps the dedicated `memorytree` branch as the shared source of truth and treats your development directory as a local cache mirror.
Its recommended defaults are `memorytree`, `heartbeat_interval = "5m"`, `auto_push = true`, `generate_report = true`, and `raw_upload_permission = "not-set"`.

If you want more control, install the machine scheduler once and then register each repository with your own branch, heartbeat cadence, and report settings:

```bash
memorytree daemon install --interval 5m --auto-push true
memorytree daemon register --root . --branch memorytree-docs --heartbeat-interval 10m --auto-push false --generate-report true --report-port 10010
memorytree daemon run-once --root . --force
```

`memorytree daemon install` is the machine-level step, while `memorytree daemon quick-start --root .` is the one-command repository setup. Both are cross-platform; only the initial skill installation differs by OS.

The heartbeat now drives both transcript import and cache-mirror refresh timing. `refresh_interval` is no longer part of the runtime or config surface.

Current execution flow:

```text

apply_patch input=*** Begin Patch *** Update File: D:\demo1\memorytree-workflow\README.md @@ After the skill is linked, open any target repository and run: ```bash memorytree daemon quick-start -...
Show input payload 
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: D:\demo1\memorytree-workflow\README.md
@@
 After the skill is linked, open any target repository and run:
 
 ```bash
 memorytree daemon quick-start --root .
 ```
 
 That `quick-start` command is the same on Windows, macOS, and Ubuntu.
+
+If the shell says `memorytree` is not recognized, the skill is not on your PATH yet. Go back to the skill repository and run `npm link` again, then retry the command.
+
+Quick check:
+
+Windows PowerShell:
+
+```powershell
+Get-Command memorytree
+```
+
+macOS / Ubuntu:
+
+```bash
+command -v memorytree
+```
 
 ### Use the skill
@@
 `memorytree daemon install` is the machine-level step, while `memorytree daemon quick-start --root .` is the one-command repository setup. Both are cross-platform; only the initial skill installation differs by OS.
+
+If `memorytree` is missing in a fresh shell, fix the skill installation first and then rerun `quick-start`.
 
 The heartbeat now drives both transcript import and cache-mirror refresh timing. `refresh_interval` is no longer part of the runtime or config surface.
*** End Patch

```

That `quick-start` command is the same on Windows, macOS, and Ubuntu.

If the shell says `memorytree` is not recognized, the skill is not on your PATH yet. Go back to the skill repository and run `npm link` again, then retry the command.

Quick check:

Windows PowerShell:

```powershell
Get-Command memorytree
```

macOS / Ubuntu:

```bash
command -v memorytree
```

### Use the skill

In Claude Code, run:

```text
/memorytree-workflow
```

The skill detects whether the current repository is `not-installed`, `partial`, or `installed`, then takes the next appropriate step immediately.

### Use the CLI directly

`memorytree init` and `memorytree upgrade` only create or update repository files. They do not register the repository with heartbeat or modify `~/.memorytree/config.toml` by themselves.

Recommended first-time setup for a repository that should use heartbeat:

```bash
Tool Result #1013 shell_command call_l7h5lQA0O15vuanpT4yTMiJM exit 0 08:51:57
shell_command output=Exit code: 0 Wall time: 0.5 seconds Output: Using Node v20.20.1 Using Node v20.20.1 The heartbeat is the background automation layer. It is now project-aware and worktree-aware....
Show output payload 
Exit code: 0
Wall time: 0.5 seconds
Output:
Using Node v20.20.1
Using Node v20.20.1

The heartbeat is the background automation layer. It is now project-aware and worktree-aware.

Recommended setup:

```bash
memorytree daemon quick-start --root .
```

Use this as the quick-install path when you want the current repository to join heartbeat with the recommended defaults.

Quick Start keeps the dedicated `memorytree` branch as the shared source of truth and treats your development directory as a local cache mirror.
Its recommended defaults are `memorytree`, `heartbeat_interval = "5m"`, `auto_push = true`, `generate_report = true`, and `raw_upload_permission = "not-set"`.

If you want more control, install the machine scheduler once and then register each repository with your own branch, heartbeat cadence, and report settings:
Tool Result #1014 shell_command call_67KHyXeDbeAcEa7iPgfv2MeY exit 0 08:51:57
shell_command output=Exit code: 0 Wall time: 0.5 seconds Output: Using Node v20.20.1 Using Node v20.20.1 diff --git a/README.md b/README.md index 63b99c2..6f0f357 100644 --- a/README.md +++ b/README...
Show output payload

Using Node v20.20.1
Using Node v20.20.1
diff --git a/README.md b/README.md
index 63b99c2..6f0f357 100644
--- a/README.md
+++ b/README.md
@@ -96,6 +96,22 @@ memorytree daemon quick-start --root .
 
 That `quick-start` command is the same on Windows, macOS, and Ubuntu.
 
+If the shell says `memorytree` is not recognized, the skill is not on your PATH yet. Go back to the skill repository and run `npm link` again, then retry the command.
+
+Quick check:
+
+Windows PowerShell:
+
+```powershell
+Get-Command memorytree
+```
+
+macOS / Ubuntu:
+
+```bash
+command -v memorytree
+```
+
 ### Use the skill
 
 In Claude Code, run:
@@ -310,6 +326,8 @@ memorytree daemon run-once --root . --force
 
 `memorytree daemon install` is the machine-level step, while `memorytree daemon quick-start --root .` is the one-command repository setup. Both are cross-platform; only the initial skill installation differs by OS.
 
+If `memorytree` is missing in a fresh shell, fix the skill installation first and then rerun `quick-start`.
+
 The heartbeat now drives both transcript import and cache-mirror refresh timing. `refresh_interval` is no longer part of the runtime or config surface.

д№ӢжүҖд»ҘжҲ‘д№ӢеүҚеҠ дәҶвҖңе…ҲжЈҖжҹҘе‘Ҫд»ӨжҳҜеҗҰеӯҳеңЁвҖқпјҢжҳҜдёәдәҶеҢәеҲҶдёӨзұ»з”ЁжҲ·пјҡ
- еҸӘз”Ё skillпјҲжҜ”еҰӮ /memorytree-workflowпјүзҡ„дәәпјҢдёҚдёҖе®ҡйңҖиҰҒе…ЁеұҖ memorytree е‘Ҫд»ӨгҖӮ
- иҰҒеңЁд»»ж„ҸйЎ№зӣ®з»Ҳз«Ҝжү§иЎҢ CLI зҡ„дәәпјҢеҝ…йЎ»е…Ҳ npm linkгҖӮ

жүҖд»ҘжөҒзЁӢдёҠзҺ°еңЁеә”иҜҘжҳҜпјҡ

1. еңЁ skill д»“еә“жү§иЎҢдёҖж¬Ў npm install && npm run build && npm link
2. з”Ё Get-Command memorytreeпјҲWindowsпјүжҲ– command -v memorytreeпјҲmacOS/UbuntuпјүзЎ®и®Ө
3. еҶҚеҺ»йЎ№зӣ®йҮҢи·‘ memorytree daemon quick-start --root .

дҪ йҒҮеҲ°зҡ„жҠҘй”ҷпјҢжң¬иҙЁе°ұжҳҜз¬¬ 1 жӯҘжІЎз”ҹж•ҲеҲ°еҪ“еүҚ shellпјҲжҲ– shell иҝҳжІЎжӢҝеҲ°ж–° PATHпјүгҖӮеҰӮжһңдҪ ж„ҝж„ҸпјҢжҲ‘еҸҜд»ҘеҶҚжҠҠ README ж”№жҲҗвҖңCLI з”ЁжҲ·й»ҳи®Өе…Ҳ npm linkпјҢжЈҖжҹҘдҪңдёәз¬¬дәҢжӯҘвҖқпјҢжӣҙз¬ҰеҗҲдҪ иҜҙзҡ„вҖңзӣҙжҺҘеј•еҜјвҖқйЈҺж јгҖӮ

еҸҜд»ҘпјҢиҝҷдёӘжҖқи·ҜжҳҜеҜ№зҡ„пјҢдҪҶжңүдёӨдёӘе…ій”®зӮ№иҰҒж”№пјҡ

1. quick-start иҝҷжқЎе‘Ҫд»Өеҝ…йЎ»з»ҷ --root дёҖдёӘеҖјгҖӮ
дҪ зҺ°еңЁеҶҷзҡ„ memorytree daemon quick-start --root е°‘дәҶеҸӮж•°пјҢдјҡжҠҘй”ҷгҖӮеә”еҶҷжҲҗпјҡ

Output:
Using Node v20.20.1
Using Node v20.20.1
### Install as a skill

Install the skill once on your machine, then use it from any repository.

You need Node.js 20 or newer.

Windows PowerShell:

```powershell
git clone https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow "$env:USERPROFILE\.codex\skills\memorytree-workflow"
cd "$env:USERPROFILE\.codex\skills\memorytree-workflow"
npm install
npm run build
npm link
```

macOS:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow ~/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow
cd ~/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow
npm install
npm run build
npm link
```

Ubuntu:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow ~/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow
cd ~/.codex/skills/memorytree-workflow
npm install
npm run build
npm link
```

If you use Claude Code, replace `.codex` with `.claude` in the path above. If your skill installer expects a local path instead of a git URL, point it at the repository root. `SKILL.md` lives at the top level.

After the skill is linked, open any target repository and run:

```bash
memorytree daemon quick-start --root .
```

That `quick-start` command is the same on Windows, macOS, and Ubuntu.

If the shell says `memorytree` is not recognized, the skill is not on your PATH yet. Go back to the skill repository and run `npm link` again, then retry the command.

Quick check:

Windows PowerShell:

```powershell
Get-Command memorytree
```

macOS / Ubuntu:

```bash
command -v memorytree
```

### Use the skill

In Claude Code, run:

```text
/memorytree-workflow
```

The skill detects whether the current repository is `not-installed`, `partial`, or `installed`, then takes the next appropriate step immediately.

### Use the CLI directly

`memorytree init` and `memorytree upgrade` only create or update repository files. They do not register the repository with heartbeat or modify `~/.memorytree/config.toml` by themselves.

Recommended first-time setup for a repository that should use heartbeat:

```bash
Tool Result #1051 shell_command call_p1dTiddh6wNgqVThx8oKXFLw exit 0 08:59:03

21:  <a href="#quick-start">Quick Start</a> &nbsp;&bull;&nbsp;
53:### Install as a skill
66:npm link
76:npm link
86:npm link

97:That `quick-start` command is the same on Windows, macOS, and Ubuntu.

231:| Worktree-backed heartbeat | `daemon register` and `daemon quick-start` create an isolated MemoryTree worktree, schedule runs, and keep the shared memory branch plus development cache mirrors in sync |
311:memorytree daemon quick-start --root .
327:`memorytree daemon install` is the machine-level step, while `memorytree daemon quick-start --root .` is the one-command repository setup. Both are cross-platform; only the initial skill installation differs by OS.
329:If `memorytree` is missing in a fresh shell, fix the skill installation first and then rerun `quick-start`.
451:All commands are available through `node dist/cli.js <subcommand> ...`, or `memorytree <subcommand> ...` if you ran `npm link`.
467:| `memorytree daemon quick-start` | Quick install: connect the repository to heartbeat with the shared memory branch + local cache mirror defaults |

Show input payload 
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: D:\demo1\memorytree-workflow\README.md
@@
 After the skill is linked, open any target repository and run:
 
 ```bash
 memorytree daemon quick-start --root .
 ```
 
 That `quick-start` command is the same on Windows, macOS, and Ubuntu.
+
+End-to-end example (Windows PowerShell):
+
+```powershell
+cd C:\Users\ai\.codex\skills\memorytree-workflow
+npm install
+npm run build
+npm link
+
+cd D:\demo1\openfons
+memorytree daemon quick-start --root .
+```
+
+Notes:
+- Do not run `quick-start` inside the skill repository unless you intentionally want to register the skill repository itself.
+- `--root` must include a value such as `.` or an absolute path. `memorytree daemon quick-start --root` is incomplete and will fail.
 
 If the shell says `memorytree` is not recognized, the skill is not on your PATH yet. Go back to the skill repository and run `npm link` again, then retry the command.
*** End Patch

If you use Claude Code, replace `.codex` with `.claude` in the path above. If your skill installer expects a local path instead of a git URL, point it at the repository root. `SKILL.md` lives at the top level.

After the skill is linked, open any target repository and run:

```bash
memorytree daemon quick-start --root .
```

That `quick-start` command is the same on Windows, macOS, and Ubuntu.

End-to-end example (Windows PowerShell):

```powershell
cd C:\Users\ai\.codex\skills\memorytree-workflow
npm install
npm run build
npm link

cd D:\demo1\openfons
memorytree daemon quick-start --root .
```

Notes:
- Do not run `quick-start` inside the skill repository unless you intentionally want to register the skill repository itself.
- `--root` must include a value such as `.` or an absolute path. `memorytree daemon quick-start --root` is incomplete and will fail.

If the shell says `memorytree` is not recognized, the skill is not on your PATH yet. Go back to the skill repository and run `npm link` again, then retry the command.

Get-Command memorytree

Using Node v20.20.1
Using Node v20.20.1
If you use Claude Code, replace `.codex` with `.claude` in the path above. If your skill installer expects a local path instead of a git URL, point it at the repository root. `SKILL.md` lives at the top level.

After the skill is linked, open any target repository and run:

```bash
memorytree daemon quick-start --root .
```

That `quick-start` command is the same on Windows, macOS, and Ubuntu.

End-to-end example (Windows PowerShell):

```powershell
cd C:\Users\ai\.codex\skills\memorytree-workflow
npm install
npm run build
npm link

cd D:\path\to\your-project
memorytree daemon quick-start --root .
```

Notes:
- Do not run `quick-start` inside the skill repository unless you intentionally want to register the skill repository itself.
- `--root` must include a value such as `.` or an absolute path. `memorytree daemon quick-start --root` is incomplete and will fail.

If the shell says `memorytree` is not recognized, the skill is not on your PATH yet. Go back to the skill repository and run `npm link` again, then retry the command.

Quick check:

Windows PowerShell:

```powershell
Get-Command memorytree
```

macOS / Ubuntu:

```bash
command -v memorytree
```

### Use the skill

In Claude Code, run:

```text
/memorytree-workflow
```

The skill detects whether the current repository is `not-installed`, `partial`, or `installed`, then takes the next appropriate step immediately.

### Use the CLI directly

343:`memorytree daemon install` is the machine-level step, while `memorytree daemon quick-start --root .` is the one-command repository setup. Both are cross-platform; only the initial skill installation differs by OS.
